raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A general request to improve a research draft includes strengthening the section-level thesis, contribution framing, and methodological motivation.","R1.2":"Improving the draft centrally involves checking whether the methodological sequence and evaluative conclusions follow coherently.","R1.3":"Because the draft reports performance numbers and comparative claims, improvement includes ensuring claims match the reported evidence.","R1.5":"The draft has section-level repetition and narrative-order issues, making structural reorganization a central responsibility.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation most directly asks for improving the prose quality of the draft.","RX.1":"Any projected improvement responsibility must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the provided excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The projection must include avoiding inflated claims about reliability, competitiveness, or validation beyond the stated evidence.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities rather than performing external validation or rewriting the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection must account for avoiding downstream scholarly harm from misleading scientific or methodological statements.","RX.5":"The projection must preserve traceability to the provided draft and its stated claims, citations, and reported results."}}

rationale.R1.1: A general request to improve a research draft includes strengthening the section-level thesis, contribution framing, and methodological motivation.
rationale.R1.2: Improving the draft centrally involves checking whether the methodological sequence and evaluative conclusions follow coherently.
rationale.R1.3: Because the draft reports performance numbers and comparative claims, improvement includes ensuring claims match the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: The draft has section-level repetition and narrative-order issues, making structural reorganization a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation most directly asks for improving the prose quality of the draft.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected improvement responsibility must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the provided excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must include avoiding inflated claims about reliability, competitiveness, or validation beyond the stated evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities rather than performing external validation or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must account for avoiding downstream scholarly harm from misleading scientific or methodological statements.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must preserve traceability to the provided draft and its stated claims, citations, and reported results.
